_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket index, comprising an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects notable but uneven price movements among the components rather than a steady climb across all items. The increase is relatively recent and uneven, highlighting volatility rather than a sustained trend. The index aggregates diverse soft commodities, which can experience sharp fluctuations.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015, rising by 99%, making it the strongest driver within the basket. Orange juice prices increased by 34%, followed by wheat at 19% and sugar at 13%. These changes are primarily due to supply disruptions caused by weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes for orange juice, and frost or drought affecting arabica coffee regions—rather than broad-based inflationary pressures. Soft commodities generally trade in thin markets, where limited supply and climatic factors cause sharper price swings.
notes.why: The headline rise in the basket is driven largely by a few weather-sensitive and disease-affected crops rather than generalized market trends. Because of these vulnerabilities, inputs in these commodities may remain elevated or volatile for extended periods, reflecting ongoing supply risks in soft commodity markets.
